summary: Liu Bei; at Fucheng with Liu Zhang. Pang Tong, Fa Zheng and Wei Yan press him to seize Liu Zhang at the banquet - he refuses on principle. Receives Sun Quan's letter via Lady Sun requesting her return; Lady Sun takes Liu Shan with her until Zhao Yun intercepts on the river.
Liu Zhang; meets Liu Bei at Fucheng for the formal alliance; gives him 30,000 troops and provisions before sending him north against Zhang Lu. The chapter that hands Liu Bei his army.
Pang Tong; presses Liu Bei to assassinate Liu Zhang at the banquet; Wei Yan's sword-dance is meant as the cover. Liu Bei stops it; the chance is lost.
Fa Zheng; backs Pang Tong's assassination plan; sets up the banquet.
Wei Yan; performs the sword-dance meant to bring down Liu Zhang; foiled by Liu Bei's intervention.
Wei Yan (duplicate record).
Zhang Ren; Liu Zhang's senior commander; reads the plot and his own counter-dance with Liu Feng prevents the assassination.
Gao Pei; Liu Zhang officer guarding Fu Pass.
Yang Huai; Liu Zhang officer guarding Fu Pass with Gao Pei.
Liu Feng; joins the counter-dance with Zhang Ren that protects Liu Zhang at the banquet.
Liu Shan; taken by Lady Sun toward Wu; intercepted on the river by Zhao Yun and Zhang Fei.
Zhao Yun; intercepts Lady Sun's boat on the river and forcibly retrieves Liu Shan from her - the second of the famous baby-rescues.
Zhang Fei; arrives at the river intercept just after Zhao Yun; bullies Lady Sun's escort with his halberd.

Lady Sun; called home by her brother Sun Quan via a forged-illness letter from Lady Wu; tries to take Liu Shan with her and is forced to surrender him to Zhao Yun on the river. Returns to Wu without her husband - the marriage effectively ends.
Lady Sun (duplicate record).
Lady Wu; her name forged on the fake illness letter - Sun Quan's pretext to recall Lady Sun.
Sun Quan; engineers Lady Sun's recall; Zhang Zhao designs the trick.
Zhang Zhao; designs the forged-illness letter to lure Lady Sun home.
Zhang Zhao (duplicate record).
Zhang Hong; with Sun Quan at Wu.
Zhang Hong (duplicate record).
Zhang Hong (duplicate record).
Gu Yong; with Sun Quan.
Cao Cao; in this chapter attacks Sun Quan at Ruxu - the first Ruxu campaign. Repulsed by the southern fleet.
Cao Hong; with Cao Cao at Ruxu.
Cao Hong (duplicate record).
Xu Chu; Cao Cao's bodyguard at Ruxu.
Cheng Yu; advises Cao Cao.
Cheng Yu (duplicate record).
Xun Yu; in Xuchang - dies later this chapter, sent an empty box by Cao Cao when he opposes the Duke-of-Wei elevation; takes poison.
Xun Yu (duplicate record).
Xun Yu (duplicate record).
Xun Yun; Xun Yu's son; named in the lineage at Xun Yu's death.
Dong Zhao; pushes the Duke-of-Wei nine bestowments proposal at court.
Liang Da; minor figure named in passing.
Zhang Lu; receives word of Liu Bei's army gathering on his southern border.
Zhu Guang; Wu officer at Ruxu.
Chen Wu; with Sun Quan at Ruxu.
Han Dang; with Sun Quan at Ruxu.
Pan Zhang; with Sun Quan at Ruxu.
Zhou Tai; with Sun Quan at Ruxu.
Zhou Tai (duplicate record).
Zhuge Liang; in Jingzhou - receives Liu Bei's reports from the west.
Shentu Pan; minor figure named in passing.
Liu Biao; named in passing - long dead.
Zhang Liang the Han strategist; cited in literary aside.
Zhang Liang (duplicate record).
Liu Bei; takes Fu Pass by Pang Tong's stratagem - Yang Huai and Gao Pei lured out under flag of peace and killed. Liu Bei now in open war with Liu Zhang; pushes deeper into Yi Province.
Yang Huai; murdered at the parley by Liu Bei's officers - Pang Tong's plan.
Gao Pei; killed alongside Yang Huai at the parley.
Pang Tong; designs the parley-trap that takes Fu Pass; chapter's defining stratagem.
Fa Zheng; with Liu Bei in the western campaign.
Liu Zhang; sends Liu Bei the false letter recalling him; reads Liu Bei's response and accepts that war is now open. Dispatches Liu Gui and Zhang Ren against him.
Liu Gui; sent by Liu Zhang against Liu Bei.
Zhang Ren; commands Liu Zhang's main field army against Liu Bei.
Huang Zhong; commands Liu Bei's van; the chapter's standout duelist.
Wei Yan; commands Liu Bei's left wing in the field battles.
Wei Yan (duplicate record).
Guan Ping; with Liu Bei in the campaign.
Liu Feng; with Liu Bei in the campaign.
Meng Da; with Liu Bei in the campaign.
Meng Da (duplicate record).
Wu Lan; Liu Zhang officer.
Wu Yi; Liu Zhang officer; will defect to Liu Bei.
Lei Tong; Liu Zhang officer.
Huo Jun; minor figure named in passing.
Liu Ba; recluse scholar named in passing - will join Liu Bei's court later.
Liu Ba (duplicate record).
Liu Xun; named in passing.
Liu Xun (duplicate record).
Liu Xun (duplicate record).
Huang Quan; Liu Zhang's loyal officer; opposes Liu Bei's invasion all the way.
Peng Yang; recluse who saves Liu Bei's camp from Liu Gui's planned river-flood ambush; recruited to Liu Bei's service.
Zhang Song; secretly in correspondence with Liu Bei; his treason will be exposed in chapter 63.
Zhang Song (duplicate record).
Zhang Su; Zhang Song's brother; intercepts the treasonous letter and exposes him to Liu Zhang.
Cao Cao; off-stage in the north.
Liu Biao; named in passing.
Zhang Zhao; with Sun Quan.
Zhang Zhao (duplicate record).
Sun Quan; off-stage.
Zhang Lu; off-stage at Hanzhong.
Zhuge Liang; at Jingzhou.

Pang Tong dies at Falling Phoenix Slope - killed by an arrow ambush in a narrow defile that the locals called 'Falling Phoenix' (his own style being Young Phoenix). The omen predicted his death. Liu Bei calls Zhuge Liang from Jingzhou.
Liu Bei; loses Pang Tong; calls Zhuge Liang to take over the campaign. The chapter that turns the Yi Province campaign serious.
Zhang Ren; sets the Falling Phoenix Slope ambush that kills Pang Tong.

Zhang Fei; the famous Bazhou campaign - takes the city by ruse, captures Yan Yan in single combat, releases him on the strength of his courage and turns him into an ally. Marches through Sichuan unopposed thereafter.

Yan Yan; Bazhou's elderly defender; captured by Zhang Fei but defies execution with the famous line 'in our land we have heroes who lose their heads, not heroes who surrender'. Zhang Fei is so impressed he frees and recruits him; Yan Yan then opens every garrison along the Sichuan road.
Yan Yan (duplicate record).
Zhuge Liang; leaves Jingzhou with Zhao Yun and Zhang Fei to reinforce Liu Bei; assigns Guan Yu to hold Jingzhou. The split that will eventually cost Shu the eastern half of its empire.
Guan Yu; assigned by Zhuge Liang to hold Jingzhou with the famous 'north oppose Cao Cao, east ally with Sun Quan' instruction. The post he will hold until chapter 76.
Zhao Yun; rides with Zhuge Liang on the relief march west.
Huang Zhong; with Liu Bei in the western campaign.
Wei Yan; with Liu Bei in the western campaign.
Wei Yan (duplicate record).
Guan Ping; carries Zhuge Liang's deployment letter to Guan Yu at Jingzhou.
Fa Zheng; with Liu Bei in the western campaign.
Lei Tong; Liu Zhang officer.
Liao Hua; with Liu Bei in the western campaign.
Liu Feng; with Liu Bei.
Liu Gui; Liu Zhang officer.
Liu Zhang; exposes and executes Zhang Song after intercepting his treasonous letter via Zhang Su.
Ma Liang; with Liu Bei.
Mi Fang; at Jingzhou with Guan Yu.
Mi Zhu; at Jingzhou with Guan Yu.
Peng Yang; with Liu Bei's army.
Wu Lan; Liu Zhang officer.
Wu Yi; Liu Zhang officer.
Jian Yong; with Liu Bei.
Jiang Wan; recommended into Liu Bei's circle in this chapter - long-term Shu administrator.
Yi Ji; with Liu Bei.
Zhang Song; killed by Liu Zhang when the treasonous letter is intercepted.
Zhang Song (duplicate record).
Sun Quan; off-stage.
Cao Cao; off-stage.
Shentu Pan; minor figure named in passing.
Zhang Ren; finally caught by Zhuge Liang's bridge-trap stratagem at Luocheng's outskirts; refuses to surrender to Liu Bei and is executed. The last great Liu-Zhang field commander.
Zhuge Liang; designs the Luocheng bridge trap that captures Zhang Ren; coordinates the multi-prong assault on the city.
Liu Bei; arrives at Luocheng and presses the siege; refuses to execute Zhang Ren but cannot persuade him to defect.
Huang Zhong; commands part of the Luocheng siege.
Wei Yan; commands part of the Luocheng siege.
Wei Yan (duplicate record).
Zhang Fei; arrives at Luocheng after the Bazhou sweep with Yan Yan in tow.
Zhao Yun; with Zhuge Liang at the Luocheng siege.
Yan Yan; arrives at Luocheng with Zhang Fei after opening every garrison.
Yan Yan (duplicate record).
Liu Zhang; sends his son and Zhang Ren's last reinforcements; loses them all. Sends to Zhang Lu in desperation.
Liu Gui; killed in the Luocheng campaign.
Lei Tong; killed in the Luocheng campaign.
Wu Lan; defects to Liu Bei after the Luocheng fall.
Wu Yi; defects to Liu Bei after the Luocheng fall; his sister will become Liu Bei's empress.
Huang Quan; Liu Zhang's loyal officer; counsels against bringing Zhang Lu in for help.
Zhang Lu; receives Liu Zhang's plea for help; sends Ma Chao south with 20,000 troops in return for promises of land - the western intervention that opens chapter 65.
Ma Chao; sent by Zhang Lu south to help Liu Zhang against Liu Bei - sets up his final encounter with Zhang Fei.
Ma Dai; rides south with Ma Chao.
Pang De; named in passing - too ill to ride south with Ma Chao.
Yang Bo; minor Yi-Province officer named in passing.
Yang Fu; back in Liang Province raising troops against Ma Chao's western foothold.
Wei Kang; named in passing.
Yan Pu; Zhang Lu officer.
Cao Cao; off-stage.
Xiahou Yuan; sent against Ma Chao's western remnants.
Liu Xun; named in passing.
Liu Xun (duplicate record).
Liu Xun (duplicate record).
Fa Zheng; with Liu Bei.
Li Yan; defects to Liu Bei after the Luocheng fall - a key Shu administrator.
Li Yan (duplicate record).
Fei Guan; Liu Zhang officer who defects after Luocheng.
Dong He; Liu Zhang official.
Dong He (duplicate record).
Zhang Yi; defects to Liu Bei.
Zhang Yi (duplicate record).
Zhang Yi (duplicate record).
Zhang Yi (duplicate record).
Zhang Yi (duplicate record).
Zhang Yi (duplicate record).
Zhang Ni; defects to Liu Bei.
Jiang Ji; named in passing.
Jiang Xu; minor figure named in passing.
Jiang Xu (duplicate record).
Gu Ti; minor figure named in passing.
Liang Kuan; minor figure named in passing.
Sun Chen; minor figure named in passing.
Lady Yang; minor figure named in passing.
Yin Feng; minor figure named in passing.
Zhao Ang; loyal officer in the Liang Province uprising against Ma Chao.
Zhao Qu; minor figure named in passing.
Zhao Yue; minor figure named in passing.
Zheng Du; named in passing.
Zhuo Ying; minor figure named in passing.
Shentu Pan; minor figure named in passing.

Ma Chao; fights Zhang Fei at Jiameng Pass - the famous all-day single combat that lasts well over 200 bouts with neither able to gain advantage. Zhuge Liang then turns him through a forged letter; Ma Chao defects to Liu Bei and is the threat that finally forces Liu Zhang's surrender.

Zhang Fei; the day-long duel with Ma Chao at Jiameng Pass - torch-lit at night, riding three horses to exhaustion. Recognises Ma Chao as his equal; once Zhuge Liang turns Ma Chao to defection, the brothers become allies.

Zhuge Liang; ends the Ma Chao threat by turning him: arranges for Pang De's brother Pang Rou (in Liu Bei's service) to plant a forged letter implicating Zhang Lu's officer Yang Bo. Zhang Lu disowns Ma Chao; Liu Bei welcomes him; Liu Zhang surrenders shortly after seeing Ma Chao's banner at Chengdu's gates.

Liu Bei; welcomes Ma Chao into his army; rides to Chengdu and accepts Liu Zhang's surrender. Becomes Imperial Protector of Yi Province - his territory now spans Jingzhou + Yi, the Longzhong Plan two-thirds complete.
Liu Zhang; surrenders Chengdu and Yi Province to Liu Bei after Ma Chao's defection; sent honourably to Gongan to live out his days.
Zhang Wei; Zhang Lu's brother; the chapter's failed defender of Hanzhong's southern approaches.
Zhang Wei (duplicate record).
Zhang Wei (duplicate record).
Zhang Lu; bans Ma Chao after the forged letter from Zhuge Liang reaches him.
Ma Dai; defects with Ma Chao to Liu Bei.
Pang De; remains with Zhang Lu (and eventually Cao Cao); does not defect with Ma Chao.
Yang Bo; the Zhang Lu officer Zhuge Liang's forged letter implicates.
Cao Cao; off-stage.
Huang Zhong; with Liu Bei at the conclusion of the Yi campaign.
Wei Yan; with Liu Bei.
Wei Yan (duplicate record).
Zhao Yun; with Liu Bei at Chengdu.
Guan Yu; off-stage at Jingzhou.
Guan Ping; carries dispatches between Jingzhou and Chengdu.
Liu Feng; with Liu Bei at Chengdu.
Fa Zheng; senior adviser at the Chengdu settlement; pushes Liu Bei to take the lordship of Yi Province formally.
Huang Quan; eventually defects to Liu Bei after Liu Zhang surrenders.
Jiang Wan; in Liu Bei's circle at Chengdu.
Ma Liang; in Liu Bei's circle at Chengdu.
Ma Su; in Liu Bei's circle at Chengdu - first formal appointment.
Deng Zhi; recruited into Liu Bei's circle - long-term Shu diplomat.
Fei Yi; recruited into Liu Bei's circle - long-term Shu administrator.
Fei Shi; minor figure named in passing.
Fei Guan; defects to Liu Bei.
Li Hui; in Liu Bei's circle at Chengdu.
Li Hui (duplicate record).
Li Yan; in Liu Bei's circle.
Li Yan (duplicate record).
Huo Jun; in Liu Bei's circle.
Liao Hua; with Liu Bei.
Liu Ba; finally accepts office under Liu Bei after Chengdu falls.
Liu Ba (duplicate record).
Meng Da; with Liu Bei at Chengdu.
Meng Da (duplicate record).
Sun Qian; with Liu Bei.
Sun Qian (duplicate record).
Mi Fang; at Jingzhou with Guan Yu.
Mi Zhu; with Liu Bei.
Yi Ji; with Liu Bei.
Peng Yang; in Liu Bei's circle.
Jian Yong; with Liu Bei.
Lei Tong; named in passing - already dead.
Lu Yin; minor figure named in passing.
Liu Jun; minor figure named in passing.
Liu Jun (duplicate record).
Qiao Zhou; recruited into Liu Bei's circle - long-term astrologer/historian.
Qin Mi; recruited into Liu Bei's circle.
Wu Lan; with Liu Bei.
Wu Yi; with Liu Bei; sister marries Liu Bei to become his queen.
Lady Xi; Wu Yi's sister; will become Liu Bei's queen after Lady Gan's death.
Xu Jing; senior scholar in the Chengdu court.
Yang Hong; in Liu Bei's circle.
Yang Hong (duplicate record).
Sun Quan; sends Lu Su to demand Jingzhou's return at chapter's end - the setup for chapter 66.
Gu Yong; with Sun Quan.
Yan Pu; Zhang Lu officer named in passing.
Yan Yan; recruited to Liu Bei's service after Bazhou.
Yan Yan (duplicate record).
Shentu Pan; minor figure named in passing.
Dong He; senior Yi-Province official; defects to Liu Bei after Chengdu surrenders.
Dong He (duplicate record).
Pang Tong; named in passing - already dead at Falling Phoenix Slope.
Zhang Ni; senior Liu Zhang officer; defects to Liu Bei.
Zhang Yi; defects to Liu Bei after Chengdu falls.
Zhang Yi (duplicate record).
Zhang Yi (duplicate record).
Zhang Yi (duplicate record).
Zhang Yi (duplicate record).
Zhang Yi (duplicate record).
Zhang Zhao; with Sun Quan.
Zhang Zhao (duplicate record).
Zheng Du; Liu Zhang officer who proposed scorched-earth - overruled; mentioned in the surrender recap.
Zhou Qun; Liu Zhang official who defects to Liu Bei.
Zhuo Ying; minor figure named in passing.
